reviews.review_text: A relaxing little time-waster. Great on the Steam Deck. This game makes airplane rides feel way too quick!
very good game love this game long time ago i played this cracked with my friend now i can buy thw wholw bundle keep it up devs hope there willl be more contents

Outstanding visuals, sound and music. Gameplay is easy to grasp, satisfying and rewarding, good replayability. Offers great gameplay for casual players and a decent amount of things for completionists. The togglable difficulty is perfect whether you want to play very relaxed and just enjoy the beautiful gameplay and ambience, and offers a great challenge on higher difficulty. 
The Norse lands dlc is a chefs kiss from me, highly recommend
Fantastic little game. I've been playing since the original Kingdom came out years ago and it's only ever gotten better and more refined over time. My go to game when travelling.
Incredibly addicting, and fun to play!
over 100 hours and just now i started to play on normal mode after beating all DLCs on easy mode. the visuals on this game are exquisite

Its pretty fun, need to do more research on it. But wow is it not fun not knowing what you are doing, left a major island before moving onto a new one but its taking me ages to get the boat so now my island will be decayed a lot.  It's a goodish game.
Quite a relaxing game, but took me a while to figure things out. Worth it in the end.
I never thought I'd be so addicted to a game with 4 controls.  Great job, worth every gold coin.

A nice side‑scrolling strategy game where every coin matters.  
Build, explore, defend, repeat.. The art looks good, the soundtrack is cozy, and the “simple but deep” gameplay loop is perfect for both short- and long sessions.
Recommended if you like calm strategy with a bit of pressure.
I'm addicted tbh The worlds are gorgeous, the music is perfect, truly a wonderful set of games

I really enjoy this game, I've completed it multiple times on all the difficulty levels and its always a good time. I like that it's not very punishing when you lose so you can play it pretty casually if you want but it has the insentives to play more intense if thats what you prefer

The best DLC for this game by far. Shakes up the end game which i felt was always needed. 

A little bit buggy but totally worth. 

Make MORE DLC's like this!!!

I loved this!

I got completely lost in the vibes, the art, the music, everything! Something about it just kept tugging at me to come back. The best way I can describe it is a side-scrolling city builder with RTS (kinda) elements. Try it out, it's on sale all the time. You will NOT be disappointed.
Still figuring things out and already hooked. Every run teaches you something new. Gorgeous, chill, and surprisingly addictive — absolutely worth it.

Pretty good, chill co-op game. Great for an easy laid back day. Just enough challenge to keep it engaging though.

Negatives:
Might be tedious on waves after portals are destroyed when you have like 6 of the big guys, but we kinda cheesed it with the Griffin and lizard. The game does get a little repetitive by the end. Also winter is absolutely brutal if you move to another island just before it starts, almost bad enough to ruin the game but we persevered.
Played this game with my sister and it is one of our favorite games, the gameplay loop in simple but fun and the DLC is fantastic especially Call of Olympus.
A really cool and relaxed game. I love the way it gets you into the "game zone" and refuses to let go. Like a good meditation practice.
fun to play with friends until they cry that its too boring and never hop on again
Fun game, not much in the way of guidance, but still fun

This is the game I use to relax. The music i great, the environment is soothing and I have played this game soo many hours that it is does not require too much brain power to play. Still I mess up and is not the best player but it is failry easy and forgiving.
Pretty chill game with a satysfing loop. Lovely pixel art, soundtrack and sound design are great

Two Crowns (base game) is a beautiful, addictive game, but I felt it suffered from repetitive gameplay and lacking content and is, at times, rather slow as well. Its entertainment value starts high, yet drops quickly after the first 10 or so hours. Theres's also no pay-off for the ending. A lot hinges on the rather excellent art and sound design to be honest.

I would still recommend it though, just bear in mind the above and consider waiting for a discount.
Very light on stratege. It is fun to play with your girlfriend who likes games, but is not a gamer.

The relaxed play at your own pace, the relaxing background music.   Figuring out what to do by yourself without the game explaining anything but the basics is so much fun.  Yes, kindgom 80's tried to take a new direction and was very creative and I like some of those concepts.  But ended up taking away from flow of the original and the sequel.  Call of olympus brings that back, it also adds some new twists with the bosses that i really enjoyed as well.

For the record: I would put this game somewhere in the middle, but seeing that Steam doesn't allow that for review, I'm going to lean more towards the negative. While there is a lot to enjoy about this game: the pixel graphics are lovely, the music is great, and the overall gameplay is addictive and fun. 

 But ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, this is easily the most unforgiving games I have ever played. If you make even the smallest mistake you are just completely screwed and you’ll have no choice but to reset. The Greed are the absolute worst, and there is literally just no room for error in this damn game. ANY mistake, no matter how tiny, will cost you absolutely everything. There is no rebuilding or anything like that, you just have to reset and try not to scream.

Also, there’s the boat. 

I honestly have NO IDEA why the developers thought this was smart. Every single time you use the boat to go to a new land, the boat with fall apart and you have to rebuild it.

EVERY.

SINGLE. 

TIME. 

This mechanic screws you over so bad. It cost so much money and time to fix it, so if you travel to a new land and get overwhelmed and decide to go back to a previous one: well too bad! You have to rebuild your boat! Oh, you ran out of money trying to defend your home against the bullsh*t enemies? Well, I guess you’ll just have to completely reset the game and start over! Isn’t this fun???

Seriously though, I lost count how many times I had to reset the game from the beginning because I got put into a situation where I literally had no other choice. It wasn't until nearly 40 hours of grinding did I finally reach a point where I can play the game comfortably, but even then that's only because I had to go to reddit for help because the game itself barely tells you what to do. The tutorial is super annoying and it's overall just very unclear on what certain things do. 

So if I had to rate this game on a five star rating, I would probably put it at 2 and a half or 3. But seeing that I can only rate it positive or negative, I'll have to say negative. Maybe that will change the more I play, but I found myself regretting my purchase a few times.

You are standing next to a line of 10 cardboard boxes.  You have a bag of quarters.  Drop a quarter in one box and it gives you more quarters.  Drop a quarter in another box and a new box gets added to the line.  Do that for two hours until there are 100 boxes in the line.

If that sounds like fun, this game is for you.

i have been playing this game since 2019 and its absolutely wonderful solo or COOP. 
one of the most brilliant indi games of our time.

[h1]Fun and intuitive co-op game[/h1]
This was fun going into with both players having little or no pre-knowledge about the game. There are no explanations or info boxes to read that explain the mechanics so everything has to be intuited. 

Building up a town together in co-op is fun! A full campaign lasts [b]~12-15 hours[/b]. The difficulty curve is fairly smooth and overall very well crafted.

If you're an achievement junkie: Beware that 70% of achievements will [u]only unlock for the host[/u] and not "Player 2". If only one person cares about achievements then they must host. Otherwise. you can solve it by having "Player 2" host the next full campaign.

Quite polished, and very pleasing to play generally. However, building does suffer from the lack of a destruction mechanic; you cannot go back on any mistakes when building, nor can you try different buildings once you've already built them, unless you wait a couple hundred days for them to decay while you're on another island, which is obviously pointless in most cases.

Mostly, I only dislike that fact because I want to make my island as pretty as possible, and sometimes I screw up and build something that makes the place less orderly. Alas.

Boring - I'm just walking left and right, pressing down to put coins in items in the landscape, that's ALL you do! it's absolute garbage gameplay wise - I'm genuinely amazed this is being recognised as a game, it's a joke!

Excessively unfair, in a bad way.  The gameplay is bashing your head against a brick wall until you find the correct brick, without guidance. For what it's worth, the art/sound/music are perfectly fine or even great.

Here are some core design principles that run the game:
- Unforgiving.
- Slow / long.
- Big progress loss as punishment.
- No hints or info about almost literally anything.

Separately, not necessarily bad design principles. But combined? TERRIBLE. You are basically funneled into failure due to knowledge checks, over and over. This is most of the game. This makes such punishment and time loss totally uncalled for.

You aren't losing because you're making bad calculations; trying to figure out something complicated; failing to be clever under pressure, nor other reasons you'd hope you lose a fair game. You are losing because you didn't read a wiki, or obsess about obtuse npc programming that makes no sense unless you read about it. Or you pressed the interact button after creating a boat, and instantly lose because nothing warns you it will steal you away without asking and you had to bring workers and money. You refuse to even give a message/warning confirming I want to get on the boat, when it can easily be fatal and we don't know what it does?

Basically every step of the game is rigged against you, and the only way you will like it is if you don't care when a game's entire goal is to sucker punch you repeatedly with no input. The only way you'll enjoy this is if you don't mind repeatedly losing an hour of your life because the game refuses to explain basic rules until you learn by dying to lack of information.

Devs clearly take pride in refusing to add any UI or text to the game. They want you to enjoy the mysterious and get excited over new discoveries. As cool as that can be, in this case it just forces you to grind out fundamental information for too long, like trying to play an rpg in a language you don't know. It's like playing I Wanna Be The Guy, dying to a totally invisible death trap, and then it makes you start the game over instead of respawning you.

I can give one major caveat that turns the review positive. If you are like me and unwilling to experience sucker punches and unnecessary lack of information, playing with an experienced friend will skip much of bad part of the game. Of course you will be missing out on the discovery aspect, but I think it was done so underhandedly it's not a huge loss.

There are people who can enjoy a rigged game and grind out the answer to the equation, but it's not me.
The best in the series, but it gets boring after the third island
Best title for couch gaming with your significant other!

⊹ ࣪ ˖ **Recommended** ˖ ࣪ ⊹

Kingdom Two Crowns is one of the first games I ever bought on Steam back when I had a laptop, so it feels like the start of my whole Steam journey. Because of that, this game will always have a special place in my heart. ⋆˚꩜｡

I love this game so much. There is something so satisfying about slowly building up your kingdom, giving coins to the poor little wanderers, hiring them, and watching your tiny kingdom grow into something stronger. Lmao, I love throwing money at people and being like, “You work for me now.” ✧

The gameplay is simple at first, but it gets really addicting. You collect coins, build walls, upgrade towers, hire archers, farmers, and knights, and try to survive each night while the Greed come crawling out to attack. Every day feels like you are preparing, and every night feels like a little test to see if your kingdom can hold. ˚₊‧꒰ა ☆ ໒꒱ ‧₊˚

I also love exploring the different islands. Each island makes you think about how to build, where to spend your coins, what to upgrade first, and how risky you want to be. Sometimes you feel powerful, and sometimes you realize you made one tiny bad choice and now everything is falling apart. Very humbling. ⟡

The mounts are also so fun. I especially love the mount that poops coins, lmao. It is silly, useful, and honestly iconic. The game has such a pretty art style too, with the pixel graphics, the changing seasons, the water reflections, and the quiet little moments while you ride around your kingdom. ⋆˙⟡

And then there was my dog, Mr. Dog. I loved him. I protected him. I fought so hard to keep him safe. Then he got taken away, and honestly? Never again. That betrayal hurt. I will never emotionally recover from Mr. Dog being stolen from me. ✨

Overall, Kingdom Two Crowns is calm, stressful, pretty, and chaotic all at once. It is a fun kingdom-building survival game with exploration, strategy, cute mounts, scary nights, and enough tiny heartbreak to keep you attached.

Also, protect your dog. Always. ✨

- Nice visuals
- Great story
- Intriguing varieties

Can't recommend. Was playing with wife on easy difficulty and all of sudden one night there was 100 times more mosters than ever before and they just absolutely wiped us out in seconds. Game breaking bug or devs don't understand what easy is...
